title: Refactor existing KERNEL-003 sandbox consumers to use SandboxAdapter trait
goal: Move every call site in the shipped KERNEL-003 codebase that today consumes the concrete DocketAdapter type onto the abstract `Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>` trait. After this MT, no module in src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/** or src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/** imports the KERNEL-003 DocketAdapter type directly; all consumption flows through the trait via dependency injection.
expected_diff_shape: Mechanical refactor across the KERNEL-003 consumer modules (run Grep `DocketAdapter|docket_adapter|DockerRunner` across src/backend/handshake_core/src/** at MT-claim time to locate; expected affected files ~5-12); each `Arc<DocketAdapter>` field becomes `Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>`; constructors gain SandboxAdapter injection; no behavior change. Migration log artifact at .GOV/roles_shared/records/sandbox/WP-KERNEL-004-kernel-003-callsite-migration.json capturing every (file, line, before, after) for the refactor.
unblock_conditions: DockerAdapter (MT-047) is the migration target for existing callers; selection policy (MT-043) provides default; ValidationRunner binding (MT-050) is the model for how to inject.
implementation_notes: Pre-step: Grep `DocketAdapter`, `docket_adapter`, `DockerRunner`, and `docker_runner` across src/backend/handshake_core/src/**. Expected hits: KERNEL-003 sandbox runner module, KERNEL-001 ToolGate (if it touches sandbox), KERNEL-001 PromotionGate (if it touches sandbox), tests fixtures. Record exact files in the migration log. For each call site: (1) change field type from concrete DocketAdapter to `Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>`; (2) change constructor parameter; (3) if construction happens inline (`Arc::new(DocketAdapter::new(...))`), move to the bootstrap layer (MT-048) — pass the constructed adapter into the constructor instead; (4) update direct method calls — DocketAdapter likely has `run(image, cmd, binds, env)` shape; map onto SandboxAdapter::spawn + the docker-specific argv mapping that now lives in src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/docker/adapter.rs (MT-047). Behavior parity discipline: NO functional change in this MT — the refactor is purely structural. To prove parity, the migration log records every changed call site AND a paired before/after test that asserts the same input produces the same OS-level behavior (assert the resulting docker argv via the parity fixture from MT-047). Care with backwards-compatibility: KERNEL-003 callers in main may construct DocketAdapter at multiple points; consolidating into the bootstrap layer requires propagating a SandboxAdapterRegistry handle to those construction sites. Use SessionBroker / Tauri app state (whichever KERNEL-001 already threads as shared state) as the injection vehicle to avoid passing the registry through every function. Tests: (a) compile-time test that no module outside src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/docker/ imports DocketAdapter directly (grep test); (b) each affected module's existing test suite passes unchanged; (c) migration-log schema validation (`schema: "hsk.sandbox_callsite_migration@1"`).
risk_if_missed: Without refactor, KERNEL-003 callers stay docker-only and the 3-adapter pattern cannot select WSL2Podman or WindowsNativeJail for those code paths; R-3 ('orphans KERNEL-003') remains live.
validator_focus: (a) Behavior parity: existing KERNEL-003 tests pass unchanged; (b) grep test asserts no direct DocketAdapter import outside the docker adapter module; (c) migration log is exhaustive (no surprise unchanged file imports DocketAdapter); (d) bootstrap layer is the single injection point.

minimum_controls: Behavior parity: existing KERNEL-003 test suite passes without any test modification; tests changes are themselves a red flag in this MT.
no-direct-docket-adapter-import-check.mjs prevents future KERNEL-003 callers from re-importing the concrete type after the refactor.
Migration log exhaustiveness: a final repo-wide grep at MT close verifies log.changes covers every match (audit step for the validator).
